name: Empathy Map
description: Map what users think, feel, say, do

name: User Interview
description: Structured interview with target users

name: Journey Map
description: Map end-to-end user experience

name: How Might We
description: Reframe insights as opportunity questions

name: Point of View
description: User + Need + Insight statement

name: Crazy 8s
description: 8 ideas in 8 minutes

name: SCAMPER
description: Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to use, Eliminate, Reverse

name: Mind Mapping
description: Visual idea expansion from central theme

name: Worst Idea
description: Generate deliberately terrible ideas, then invert

name: Paper Prototype
description: Quick sketched interface

name: Storyboard
description: Sequential visual narrative

name: Contextual Inquiry
description: Observe users in their natural environment while they work

name: Diary Study
description: Users log experiences over days or weeks

name: Stakeholder Map
description: Map all stakeholders and their relationships to the problem

name: Affinity Diagram
description: Cluster observations into themes and patterns

name: Problem Tree
description: Map root causes, core problem, and effects hierarchically

name: Brainwriting
description: Silent written idea generation passed between participants

name: Six Thinking Hats
description: Explore ideas from 6 perspectives: facts, emotions, caution, benefits, creativity, process

name: Random Word Stimulation
description: Use random words as creative triggers for new connections

name: Reverse Brainstorming
description: Generate ways to cause the problem, then invert

name: Dot Voting
description: Democratic prioritization of generated ideas

name: Wizard of Oz
description: Human simulates system behavior behind the scenes

name: Bodystorming
description: Physically act out scenarios to discover insights

name: Clickable Prototype
description: Interactive screen-based mockup for user testing

name: A/B Concept Test
description: Compare two approaches with users

name: Usability Testing
description: Observe users completing tasks to find friction points

name: Hallway Testing
description: Quick informal test with nearby people for fast feedback
